text.title: ADR Reversibility Tagging Worked Example
text.summary: One end-to-end worked example showing how adr-reversibility-tagging produces a valid decision-record artefact.

example.title: Happy-path application
example.code:
--- adr_id: 023 title: Migrate primary auth from Auth0 to Clerk reversibility: one_way_door_costly rollback_estimate: engineering_weeks: 4 dollars_contract_exit: 12000 customers_affected: all_active status: proposed owner: Ruslan Faion <ruslan@faion.net> ---
## Decision Switch identity provider from Auth0 to Clerk for new signups starting Q3. Existing users migrated via JIT provisioning.
## Pre-mortem 12 months from now this was wrong because: 1. Clerk's pricing climbed and TCO exceeded Auth0 by 2x. 2. Clerk's enterprise SSO blocked a key procurement.
Reversal triggers: - monthly bill / MAU > 2x projected - >= 1 enterprise sales deal blocked on SSO feature gap
example.note: Demonstrates the rules from 01-core-rules.xml applied to a concrete input; output validates against the schema in 02-output-contract.xml.
